reason: Bitcoin buyers are rapidly cashing out after failing to sustain above $74,000, signaling a potential local top and profit-taking. This, combined with broad market weakness (all CoinDesk 20 constituents lower), negative macroeconomic data (unexpected U.S. job losses leading to higher unemployment), and geopolitical instability (Trump's stance on Iran, oil surging, Bitcoin/stocks lower), points to a significant risk-off environment. Regulatory scrutiny on Binance adds further uncertainty. The confluence of these factors suggests strong downward pressure and increased volatility.
headlines: Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
'No deal with Iran': Trump demands unconditional surrender, sending oil surging, bitcoin and stocks lower
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Aave drops 4.3% as all index constituents trade lower
U.S. unexpectedly lost 92,000 jobs in February, unemployment rate rose to 4.4%
